metadata_information.title: Estimating willingness-to-pay with random valuation models : an application to Lake Sevan, Armenia
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Estimating willingness-to-pay with random valuation models : an application to Lake Sevan, Armenia

document_description.abstract: This paper presents a case study of willingness-to-pay (WTP) estimation using random valuation models. A contingent valuation survey was conducted in Yerevan, Armenia to estimate people's WTP for the protection of Lake Sevan. Three elicitation formats-open-ended, closed-ended, and the stochastic payment card (SPC) approach-were used with split random samples. WTP models with heterogeneous errors were constructed and estimated with the survey data. The SPC approach produces a higher estimation of the mean WTP than both the open-ended and closed-ended approaches, while results from the open-ended and closed-ended elicitation formats are similar. Furthermore, contrary to research findings obtained in the United States, this study finds higher WTP estimations with mail surveys than with personal interviews.
